name: test-coverage

tasks.title: Add replayJSON tests for streaming and gzip decompression
tasks.description: Add replayJSON tests for streaming and gzip decompression
tasks.body: Write tests in cmd/daemon/follow_test.go (or the appropriate test file) covering: (1) plain .jsonl files are streamed line-by-line to stdout, (2) .jsonl.gz files are decompressed and their lines appear as plain NDJSON on stdout, (3) a mixed list of plain and gzip files produces the correct concatenated output, (4) missing or unreadable files are skipped without error. Use the same gzip helper pattern as internal/logrender/reader_test.go:writeGzLines.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in test-coverage is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify tests cover plain, gzip, mixed, and missing-file scenarios

breadcrumbs.text: Added 7 replayJSON unit tests in cmd/daemon/follow_test.go covering plain JSONL streaming, gzip decompression, mixed files, missing/unreadable file skipping, and edge cases

Audit reviewed 1 file (198 new lines in cmd/daemon/follow_test.go). PASS. Seven replayJSON unit tests covering plain JSONL streaming, gzip decompression, mixed file concatenation, missing/unreadable file skipping, empty sessions, and all-missing sessions. All tests pass, go vet clean. Two minor notes: (1) writeGzLines discards gz.Write errors on lines 454-455, acceptable since gz.Close is checked and failures would surface as test assertions. (2) captureReplayJSON is not parallel-safe, correctly documented and tests omit t.Parallel(). No gaps, no escalations. Quality: clean, focused test coverage matching the production code's error-skip behavior.

audit.result_summary: Added 7 unit tests for the replayJSON streaming path in cmd/daemon/follow_test.go, covering plain JSONL output, gzip decompression, mixed plain/gz file concatenation, graceful skipping of missing and unreadable files, empty sessions, and all-missing sessions. Tests use a pipe-capture helper to verify actual stdout content without modifying production code. All tests pass, build and vet clean.

task-0001.objective: Add unit tests for replayJSON streaming and gzip decompression
task-0001.what_happened: Added 7 tests to cmd/daemon/follow_test.go covering plain .jsonl streaming, .jsonl.gz decompression, mixed file concatenation, missing file skipping, unreadable file skipping, empty sessions, and all-missing sessions. Used os.Pipe to capture stdout and verify actual output content. Added a writeGzLines helper matching the pattern from internal/logrender/reader_test.go, and a captureReplayJSON helper that swaps os.Stdout temporarily.
went_well: The pipe-capture pattern cleanly verifies output without touching the production code
Tests are not parallel because they swap os.Stdout, which keeps them correct without mutex overhead
improvements: The captureReplayJSON helper is not safe for parallel use; if future tests need parallelism with stdout capture, the production code would need an io.Writer parameter instead of writing to os.Stdout directly
action_items: Consider refactoring replayJSONFile to accept an io.Writer if more thorough output-content tests are needed elsewhere
